{{Quote|I've been having a run of bad luck, but that's all going to change.|Kirk Windjammer|<ref name="Droids 5" />}}
'''Kirk Windjammer''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]]. His profession was the shipping of goods in his [[anti-grav loader]], the ''[[Seaskimmer]]''. Upon losing a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] at sea, he purchased [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] as a replacement from droid salesman [[Van P. Quist]], separating the droid from his counterpart, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]].

After helping Prince [[Jagoda]] prevent an invasion of [[R-Duba]] from [[Dorande]], he was made the prince's royal advisor.

==Biography==
In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Kirk Windjammer went through a difficult period when he went on to purchase [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] at a store. Windjammer did not have enough credits to buy [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], and the two droids remained separated. Windjammer was sorry, but sought primarily the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] for controlling his vehicle, the ''[[Seaskimmer]]''.

Kirk and Artoo found a new job at the [[Red Asteroid Bar]]. During their journey at sea, Kirk complained about the high taxes that he was required to pay in order to trade at sea. When delivering their cargo on a Gunship, Kirk discovered that they had smuggled illegal weapons. The two escaped and reached the palace of Jagoda.

Under the direction of C-3PO, who in the meantime had become the Royal Advisor, Kirk and Artoo were encountered during an invasion. Shortly after Artoo and Threepio disappeared, Kirk was appointed the new Royal Advisor. Kirk's first advice was to never forget the droids. After that, Jagoda agreed to lower taxes and reduce the maritime trade.
